test_cases.description: Verify that every API key returned by GenerateAPIKey matches the canonical format <prefix>_<key_id>_<secret> with an 8-char key_id and 32-char secret.
preconditions: internal/keys package is compiled
TokenPrefix is set to 'ak' (default)
A valid SQLite in-memory database is available with the api_keys table created per spec 02 schema
expiresDays is set to 90
test_cases.expected: FullKey matches regex ^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$; KeyID is exactly 8 characters; FullKey split on '_' produces exactly 3 segments where segment[0] == 'ak', len(segment[1]) == 8, len(segment[2]) == 32
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-001', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
parts = split(result.FullKey, '_')
assert len(parts) == 3
assert parts[0] == 'ak'
assert len(parts[1]) == 8
assert len(parts[2]) == 32
assert matches(result.FullKey, '^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$')

test_cases.description: Verify that key_id and secret contain only alphanumeric characters from [0-9A-Za-z] and are generated using crypto/rand.
preconditions: internal/keys package is compiled
A valid SQLite in-memory database is available with the api_keys table
expiresDays is set to 30
test_cases.expected: KeyID (8 chars) and secret portion (32 chars) of FullKey contain only characters matching [0-9A-Za-z]; no characters outside the 62-char alphanumeric set are present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-002', 30, logger)
assert err == nil
keyID = result.KeyID
secret = split(result.FullKey, '_')[2]
assert matches(keyID, '^[0-9A-Za-z]{8}$')
assert matches(secret, '^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$')

test_cases.description: Verify that a crypto/rand.Read() failure causes GenerateAPIKey to return a non-nil error immediately with no retries and no database writes.
preconditions: internal/keys package is compiled with a mockable rand reader
A valid SQLite in-memory database is available with the api_keys table
crypto/rand.Read is injected/mocked to always return an error on first call
test_cases.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns a non-nil error; no rows are inserted into api_keys; no retries are attempted (mock called exactly once)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
mockRand.alwaysFail = true
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-003', 90, logger)
assert err != nil
assert result == nil
rowCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', 'user-003')
assert rowCount == 0
assert mockRand.callCount == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey accepts the correct parameter types and returns *APIKeyResult with all fields populated on success.
preconditions: internal/keys package is compiled
A valid SQLite in-memory database is available with the api_keys table
expiresDays is 90
test_cases.expected: Returns (*APIKeyResult, nil); FullKey, KeyID, SecretHash, ExpiresAt are all non-zero/non-nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-004', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
assert result != nil
assert result.FullKey != ''
assert result.KeyID != ''
assert result.SecretHash != ''
assert result.ExpiresAt != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey returns a non-nil error immediately when expiresDays is not in {0, 30, 60, 90}, without touching the database.
preconditions: internal/keys package is compiled
A valid SQLite in-memory database is available with the api_keys table
test_cases.expected: Returns (nil, non-nil error); no rows inserted into api_keys; error is a Go error (not an HTTP response)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for invalidDays in [1, 15, 45, 100, -1, 365]:
    result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-005', invalidDays, logger)
    assert err != nil
    assert result == nil
    rowCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', 'user-005')
    assert rowCount == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that when called with *sql.DB, GenerateAPIKey begins an internal transaction and rolls back atomically if the INSERT fails, leaving existing keys unmodified.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
An existing active key for user-006 is present in api_keys with revoked_at IS NULL
A mechanism to force INSERT failure exists (e.g., pre-inserted row with the generated key_id to trigger unique constraint violation with retries exhausted)
test_cases.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns a non-nil error; the pre-existing key's revoked_at remains NULL (transaction was rolled back); no new key row is present in api_keys for user-006 beyond the original
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
originalKey = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id, revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-006')
forceInsertFailure(db, 3)
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-006', 90, logger)
assert err != nil
restoredKey = db.queryRow('SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', originalKey.key_id)
assert restoredKey.revoked_at == NULL
totalRows = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', 'user-006')
assert totalRows == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that when called with *sql.Tx, GenerateAPIKey executes within the caller's transaction without beginning a nested transaction, and that rollback undoes the writes.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
No existing keys for user-007
test_cases.expected: After commit: key row is present in api_keys. After rollback: no key row is present in api_keys. In both cases GenerateAPIKey itself does not call Commit or Rollback.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// commit scenario
tx1 = db.Begin()
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(tx1, 'user-007', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
tx1.Commit()
rowCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', 'user-007')
assert rowCount == 1

// rollback scenario
tx2 = db.Begin()
result2, err2 = GenerateAPIKey(tx2, 'user-007b', 90, logger)
assert err2 == nil
tx2.Rollback()
rowCount2 = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', 'user-007b')
assert rowCount2 == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey executes the bulk revocation UPDATE before INSERT, and that zero rows affected (first-time user) is treated as a silent no-op success.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-008 has no existing keys (first-time user)
test_cases.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns (*APIKeyResult, nil); one new key is inserted; no error is raised from zero-rows-affected revocation UPDATE
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-008', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
assert result != nil
rowCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-008')
assert rowCount == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey generates correct key_id, secret, secret_hash, and inserts all fields into api_keys.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
No existing keys for user-009
test_cases.expected: result.FullKey == 'ak_' + result.KeyID + '_' + secret; result.SecretHash == lowercase hex SHA-256 of secret; api_keys row has matching key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_days=30
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-009', 30, logger)
assert err == nil
parts = split(result.FullKey, '_')
secret = parts[2]
expectedHash = hex(sha256(secret))
assert result.SecretHash == expectedHash
assert len(result.SecretHash) == 64
assert all chars in result.SecretHash are in [0-9a-f]
row = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_days FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', result.KeyID)
assert row.key_id == result.KeyID
assert row.user_id == 'user-009'
assert row.secret_hash == result.SecretHash
assert row.expires_days == 30

test_cases.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey retries key_id generation up to 3 total attempts on unique constraint violation and succeeds if a non-colliding key_id is available within the retry budget.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
No existing active key for user-010
A test harness can inject a collision on the first key_id generation attempt (e.g., pre-inserting a row with the expected first key_id, using a seeded random generator for the first call)
test_cases.expected: GenerateAPIKey succeeds (returns non-nil result, nil error) on the second attempt; exactly one new active key exists for user-010; the revocation UPDATE is not re-executed on retry
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// Pre-insert a row with the first expected key_id to force collision
preInsertedKeyID = getFirstGeneratedKeyID()
db.exec('INSERT INTO api_keys (key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_days, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)', preInsertedKeyID, 'other-user', 'hash', 90, now())
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-010', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
assert result != nil
assert result.KeyID != preInsertedKeyID
activeRows = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-010')
assert activeRows == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that ExpiresAt is nil when expiresDays=0, and is exactly N*24h from creation for expiresDays in {30,60,90}.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
No existing keys for any test user
test_cases.expected: expiresDays=0 → ExpiresAt==nil and expires_at IS NULL in DB; expiresDays=30 → ExpiresAt is approx now+720h (within 2s); expiresDays=60 → approx now+1440h; expiresDays=90 → approx now+2160h
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
before = now()
result0, _ = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-exp-0', 0, logger)
assert result0.ExpiresAt == nil
row0 = db.queryRow('SELECT expires_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', result0.KeyID)
assert row0.expires_at == NULL

for (days, hours) in [(30, 720), (60, 1440), (90, 2160)]:
    result, _ = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-exp-'+days, days, logger)
    assert result.ExpiresAt != nil
    diff = result.ExpiresAt - before
    assert diff >= hours*3600 - 2
    assert diff <= hours*3600 + 2

test_cases.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey emits a structured INFO log entry with user_id and key_id fields after a successful INSERT, and does not emit a log entry on failure.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
A test logger is injected that captures log entries
No existing keys for user-011
test_cases.expected: On success: exactly one INFO log entry is captured containing user_id='user-011' and key_id matching the returned KeyID. On forced failure (crypto/rand error): zero INFO log entries are captured.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
testLogger = newCapturingLogger()
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-011', 90, testLogger)
assert err == nil
entries = testLogger.getEntries(level=INFO)
assert len(entries) == 1
assert entries[0].fields['user_id'] == 'user-011'
assert entries[0].fields['key_id'] == result.KeyID

// failure case
testLogger2 = newCapturingLogger()
mockRand.alwaysFail = true
_, err2 = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-011b', 90, testLogger2)
assert err2 != nil
assert len(testLogger2.getEntries(level=INFO)) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that the root apikit package re-exports GenerateAPIKey as a wrapper delegating to keys.GenerateAPIKey and APIKeyResult as a type alias.
preconditions: apikit root package is compiled
internal/keys package is compiled
SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
test_cases.expected: apikit.GenerateAPIKey returns identical results to keys.GenerateAPIKey for the same inputs; apikit.APIKeyResult is assignable from keys.APIKeyResult without type conversion
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result1, err1 = apikit.GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-012', 90, logger)
assert err1 == nil
assert result1 != nil
// Verify type alias: assign without cast
var r apikit.APIKeyResult = *result1
assert r.FullKey == result1.FullKey
assert r.KeyID == result1.KeyID

test_cases.description: Verify that the apikit root package does not expose any symbols from internal/keys other than APIKeyResult and GenerateAPIKey.
preconditions: apikit root package is compiled
test_cases.expected: No custom error types (e.g., ErrKeyRevoked, ErrKeyExpired), internal constants, or helper functions from internal/keys are accessible via the apikit package namespace

test_cases.description: Verify that RegisterKeyHandlers registers exactly GET /user/keys, POST /user/keys/:key_id/refresh, and DELETE /user/keys/:key_id on the provided Echo group.
preconditions: Echo instance created with an /api/v1 group
SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
RegisterKeyHandlers called with the group and database
test_cases.expected: Three routes are registered: GET /api/v1/user/keys, POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh, DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id; requests to each route are dispatched to the correct handler
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
e = echo.New()
group = e.Group('/api/v1')
RegisterKeyHandlers(group, db)
routes = e.Routes()
assert any(r.Method=='GET' && r.Path=='/api/v1/user/keys' for r in routes)
assert any(r.Method=='POST' && r.Path=='/api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh' for r in routes)
assert any(r.Method=='DELETE' && r.Path=='/api/v1/user/keys/:key_id' for r in routes)

test_cases.description: Verify that all three endpoints respond with Cache-Control: no-store, inherited from the parent Echo group's CacheMiddleware(CacheNoStore).
preconditions: Echo instance created with /api/v1 group that has CacheMiddleware(CacheNoStore) applied
SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
RegisterKeyHandlers called
Auth middleware is applied to the group (user authenticated)
test_cases.expected: All three responses include header Cache-Control: no-store

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/user/keys returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of key metadata objects ordered by created_at DESC, never including secret or expires_days.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-013 has one active key (key_id='aB3xK9mQ') created at '2026-07-17T14:30:00Z' with expires_at='2026-10-15T14:30:00Z' and revoked_at IS NULL
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-013'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; JSON array with exactly one object containing key_id, created_at (RFC3339 UTC), expires_at (string or null), revoked_at (null); fields secret_hash, secret, expires_days are absent
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user013APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert type(body) == array
assert len(body) == 1
obj = body[0]
assert obj.key_id == 'aB3xK9mQ'
assert obj.created_at != ''
assert 'expires_at' in obj
assert 'revoked_at' in obj
assert 'secret' not in obj
assert 'secret_hash' not in obj
assert 'expires_days' not in obj

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/user/keys returns HTTP 200 with an empty JSON array [] and no ETag header when the user has no keys.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-014 has no rows in api_keys
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-014'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is exactly '[]'; no ETag header is present in the response
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user014APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body == []
assert 'ETag' not in resp.headers

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/user/keys returns all historical keys (active, expired, revoked) with no row limit, ordered by created_at DESC.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-015 has three keys: key1 (active, created most recently), key2 (revoked), key3 (expired, oldest)
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-015'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; JSON array contains all 3 keys; first element is key1 (newest created_at); last element is key3 (oldest created_at); created_at values are non-increasing across the array
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user015APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert len(body) == 3
assert body[0].key_id == key1.key_id
assert body[2].key_id == key3.key_id
for i in range(len(body)-1):
    assert parseTime(body[i].created_at) >= parseTime(body[i+1].created_at)

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/user/keys sets an ETag header and returns HTTP 304 on a subsequent request with matching If-None-Match when no changes have occurred.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-016 has one active key
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-016'
test_cases.expected: First request: HTTP 200 with ETag header set. Second request with If-None-Match matching the ETag: HTTP 304 with empty body.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user016APIKey)
assert resp1.status == 200
assert 'ETag' in resp1.headers
etag = resp1.headers['ETag']

resp2 = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user016APIKey, headers={'If-None-Match': etag})
assert resp2.status == 304
assert resp2.body == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/user/keys accepts authentication via API key or PAT with keys:read permission.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-017 has one active key
Auth middleware is configured and active
A valid PAT with keys:read permission exists for user-017
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 returned for both API key authentication and PAT with keys:read; auth middleware handles failures for unauthenticated requests

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh for an active, non-revoked, non-expired key returns HTTP 200 with the new full key, updated secret_hash, recalculated expires_at, and updated created_at in the database.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-018 has one active key with key_id='testkey1', expires_days=90, expires_at in the future, revoked_at IS NULL
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-018' with credential type=API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body contains key (FullKey string), key_id='testkey1', expires_at (recalculated); secret portion of new key is 32 alphanumeric chars; DB row has updated secret_hash, created_at, expires_at; old secret no longer validates against new secret_hash
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
oldHash = db.queryRow('SELECT secret_hash FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'testkey1').secret_hash
before = now()
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/testkey1/refresh', auth=user018APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.key_id == 'testkey1'
assert matches(body.key, '^ak_testkey1_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$')
newSecret = split(body.key, '_')[2]
newHash = hex(sha256(newSecret))
row = db.queryRow('SELECT secret_hash, created_at, expires_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'testkey1')
assert row.secret_hash == newHash
assert row.secret_hash != oldHash
assert parseTime(row.created_at) >= before
assert row.expires_at != NULL

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh returns HTTP 401 with 'API key authentication required' when authenticated via PAT.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-019 has one active key with key_id='testkey2'
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-019' with credential type=PAT
PAT has keys:manage permission
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401; response body contains error 'API key authentication required'; no database operations performed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/testkey2/refresh', auth=user019PAT)
assert resp.status == 401
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'API key authentication required'
row = db.queryRow('SELECT secret_hash FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'testkey2')
assert row.secret_hash == originalHash  // unchanged

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh returns HTTP 404 'key not found' when the key_id does not exist, without any format validation.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-020 is authenticated with API key
No key with key_id='nonexistent' exists in api_keys
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error 'key not found'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/nonexistent/refresh', auth=user020APIKey)
assert resp.status == 404
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'key not found'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh returns HTTP 404 (not 403) when the key belongs to a different user.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-A has an active key with key_id='keyA'
User user-B is authenticated via API key
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-B'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error 'key not found'; not HTTP 403
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/keyA/refresh', auth=userBAPIKey)
assert resp.status == 404
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'key not found'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh returns HTTP 400 'cannot refresh a revoked key' when the key has revoked_at set.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-021 has a key with key_id='revokedkey' and revoked_at IS NOT NULL
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-021' with credential type=API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error 'cannot refresh a revoked key'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/revokedkey/refresh', auth=user021APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'cannot refresh a revoked key'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh returns HTTP 400 'cannot refresh an expired key' when expires_at is in the past.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-022 has a key with key_id='expiredkey', revoked_at IS NULL, expires_at directly set to '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z' (past)
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-022' with credential type=API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error 'cannot refresh an expired key'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.exec('UPDATE api_keys SET expires_at = ? WHERE key_id = ?', '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z', 'expiredkey')
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/expiredkey/refresh', auth=user022APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'cannot refresh an expired key'

test_cases.description: Verify that a successful refresh emits a structured INFO log entry with user_id and key_id fields via c.Logger().
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-023 has an active key with key_id='logkey'
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-023' with credential type=API key
Test logger captures log output from Echo context
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 returned; exactly one INFO log entry captured containing user_id='user-023' and key_id='logkey'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
testLogger = newCapturingEchoLogger()
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/logkey/refresh', auth=user023APIKey, echoLogger=testLogger)
assert resp.status == 200
entries = testLogger.getEntries(level=INFO)
assert len(entries) >= 1
assert any(e.fields['user_id'] == 'user-023' && e.fields['key_id'] == 'logkey' for e in entries)

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh accepts only API key authentication, rejecting any PAT regardless of its permission scopes.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-024 has an active key
Auth middleware is configured
PATs with keys:read and keys:manage scopes exist for user-024
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401 with 'API key authentication required' for all PAT-authenticated requests; HTTP 200 only for API key authentication
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for pat in [patWithKeysRead, patWithKeysManage]:
    resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/'+keyID+'/refresh', auth=pat)
    assert resp.status == 401
    assert parseJSON(resp.body).error == 'API key authentication required'

resp2 = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/'+keyID+'/refresh', auth=user024APIKey)
assert resp2.status == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id sets revoked_at in the database and returns HTTP 200 with key_id and revoked_at.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-025 has an active key with key_id='delkey1', revoked_at IS NULL
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-025'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body is {key_id: 'delkey1', revoked_at: <RFC3339 UTC timestamp>}; api_keys row for 'delkey1' now has revoked_at set to approximately now()
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
before = now()
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/delkey1', auth=user025APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.key_id == 'delkey1'
assert body.revoked_at != null
assert parseTime(body.revoked_at) >= before
row = db.queryRow('SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'delkey1')
assert row.revoked_at != NULL

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id returns HTTP 404 'key not found' when the key_id does not match any row, without format validation.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-026 is authenticated
No key with key_id='doesnotexist' in api_keys
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error 'key not found'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/doesnotexist', auth=user026APIKey)
assert resp.status == 404
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'key not found'

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id returns HTTP 404 (not 403) when the key belongs to a different user.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-C has an active key with key_id='keyC'
User user-D is authenticated
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-D'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404; response body contains error 'key not found'; not HTTP 403
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/keyC', auth=userDAPIKey)
assert resp.status == 404
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'key not found'

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id returns HTTP 400 'key is already revoked' when the key's revoked_at is already set.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-027 has a key with key_id='alreadyrevoked' and revoked_at IS NOT NULL
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-027'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; response body contains error 'key is already revoked'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/alreadyrevoked', auth=user027APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.error == 'key is already revoked'

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id permits revoking an expired-but-not-explicitly-revoked key, setting revoked_at explicitly.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-028 has a key with key_id='expirednotrevoked', expires_at set to '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z' (past), revoked_at IS NULL
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-028'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body contains key_id='expirednotrevoked' and a non-null revoked_at; DB row has revoked_at set
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.exec('UPDATE api_keys SET expires_at = ? WHERE key_id = ?', '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z', 'expirednotrevoked')
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/expirednotrevoked', auth=user028APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body.key_id == 'expirednotrevoked'
assert body.revoked_at != null
row = db.queryRow('SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'expirednotrevoked')

test_cases.description: Verify that a successful DELETE emits a structured INFO log entry with user_id and key_id fields via c.Logger().
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-029 has an active key with key_id='logdelkey'
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-029'
Test logger captures log output from Echo context

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
testLogger = newCapturingEchoLogger()

assert resp.status == 200
entries = testLogger.getEntries(level=INFO)
assert any(e.fields['user_id'] == 'user-029' && e.fields['key_id'] == 'logdelkey' for e in entries)

test_cases.description: Verify self-revocation: a user can DELETE the key they are currently authenticating with, completing with HTTP 200, and subsequent requests with that key are rejected.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-030 has an active key with key_id='selfrevoke'
Auth middleware validates credentials at request start and does not re-validate mid-flight

test_cases.expected: First request: HTTP 200 with revoked_at set; second request with same key: HTTP 401 (rejected by auth middleware)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/selfrevoke', auth=user030ActiveAPIKey)
assert resp1.status == 200
assert parseJSON(resp1.body).revoked_at != null

resp2 = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user030ActiveAPIKey)
assert resp2.status == 401

Auth middleware is configured
A valid PAT with keys:manage permission exists for user-031

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/delkeyA', auth=user031APIKey)
assert resp1.status == 200

resp2 = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/delkeyB', auth=user031PATWithKeysManage)

test_cases.description: Verify that after a successful GenerateAPIKey call, exactly one key with revoked_at IS NULL exists for the user.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-032 already has one active key
test_cases.expected: After GenerateAPIKey completes: exactly one row in api_keys for user-032 has revoked_at IS NULL; the prior key has revoked_at set
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
oldKeyID = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-032').key_id
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-032', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
activeCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-032')
assert activeCount == 1
newActive = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-032')
assert newActive.key_id == result.KeyID
oldRow = db.queryRow('SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', oldKeyID)
assert oldRow.revoked_at != NULL

test_cases.description: Verify that refresh updates the key in-place (same key_id, same row count) rather than inserting a new row.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-033 has exactly one active key with key_id='inplacekey'
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-033' with credential type=API key
test_cases.expected: Row count in api_keys for user-033 unchanged after refresh; same key_id='inplacekey'; secret_hash, created_at, expires_at updated in-place
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/inplacekey/refresh', auth=user033APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200

assert countBefore == countAfter
row = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-033')
assert row.key_id == 'inplacekey'

test_cases.description: Verify that the one-active-key invariant is maintained under concurrent GenerateAPIKey calls via SQLite serialized writes (design-level verification).
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table in WAL or serialized mode
User user-034 has no existing keys
test_cases.expected: After all 5 concurrent GenerateAPIKey calls complete: exactly one row with revoked_at IS NULL exists for user-034
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
goroutines = []
for i in range(5):
    goroutines.append(spawn(GenerateAPIKey, db, 'user-034', 90, logger))
results = waitAll(goroutines)
assert all(r.err == nil for r in results)
activeCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-034')
assert activeCount == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that internal/keys package uses no package-level logger singleton; loggers are request-scoped via c.Logger() and explicit parameter injection.
preconditions: internal/keys package source code is available
Static analysis tooling (go vet, grep) available
test_cases.expected: No package-level var of logger type exists in internal/keys; GenerateAPIKey signature includes an explicit logger echo.Logger parameter; handlers use c.Logger()

test_cases.description: Verify that INFO log entries are emitted after successful database writes for key generation, refresh, and revocation, but not on failure paths.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
Test loggers that capture entries are injected for each operation
Users with appropriate key state set up for each scenario
test_cases.expected: Each successful lifecycle event produces at least one INFO log entry with user_id and key_id; failed operations produce zero INFO entries
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// Generation
tl1 = newCapturingLogger()
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-log-gen', 90, tl1)
assert err == nil
assert len(tl1.getEntries(INFO)) >= 1
assert tl1.getEntries(INFO)[0].fields includes {user_id, key_id}

// Refresh
tl2 = newCapturingEchoLogger()
resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/'+result.KeyID+'/refresh', echoLogger=tl2, auth=apiKey)
assert resp.status == 200
assert len(tl2.getEntries(INFO)) >= 1

// Revocation
tl3 = newCapturingEchoLogger()
resp2 = DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/'+result.KeyID, echoLogger=tl3, auth=apiKey)
assert resp2.status == 200
assert len(tl3.getEntries(INFO)) >= 1

test_cases.description: Verify that only the SHA-256 hash (lowercase 64-char hex) is stored in api_keys.secret_hash, and the plaintext secret is never written to any column.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-035 has no existing keys
test_cases.expected: api_keys row for user-035 has secret_hash == hex(sha256(secret)); no column in api_keys contains the plaintext secret; secret_hash length is 64 characters, all lowercase hex
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-035', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
secret = split(result.FullKey, '_')[2]
expectedHash = hex(sha256(secret))
row = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_days, expires_at, created_at, revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', result.KeyID)
assert row.secret_hash == expectedHash
assert len(row.secret_hash) == 64
assert matches(row.secret_hash, '^[0-9a-f]{64}$')
// Verify no column contains plaintext secret
for col in [row.key_id, row.user_id, row.expires_days, row.created_at]:
    assert secret not in str(col)

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/user/keys never includes secret_hash, plaintext secret, key, or expires_days in any response object.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-036 has one active key
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-036'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; each object in the JSON array contains ONLY key_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at; no secret, secret_hash, key, or expires_days field is present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user036APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
body = parseJSON(resp.body)
for obj in body:
    allowedKeys = {'key_id', 'created_at', 'expires_at', 'revoked_at'}
    assert set(obj.keys()) == allowedKeys
    assert 'secret' not in obj
    assert 'secret_hash' not in obj
    assert 'key' not in obj
    assert 'expires_days' not in obj

property_tests.description: For any sequence of GenerateAPIKey calls for the same user, at most one active (non-revoked, non-expired) key exists at any point.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate between 1 and 10 sequential GenerateAPIKey calls for the same userID with random valid expiresDays values from {0, 30, 60, 90}
property_tests.invariant_check: After each call, query 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now())'; assert count == 1

property_tests.description: For any row in api_keys, the secret_hash column equals hex(sha256(secret)) and no column contains the plaintext secret.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N keys (N between 1 and 20) with random valid userIDs and expiresDays values; collect all inserted rows from api_keys
property_tests.invariant_check: For each row: compute hex(sha256(secret)) from the returned FullKey; assert row.secret_hash == computed_hash; assert secret not in any string column of the row (key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_days, created_at, revoked_at)

property_tests.description: For any FullKey returned by GenerateAPIKey or the refresh endpoint, the key matches the canonical format and its constituent segments have correct lengths.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N FullKey values (N between 1 and 50) via GenerateAPIKey with random valid userIDs and expiresDays; also generate via the refresh endpoint
property_tests.invariant_check: For each FullKey: assert matches(FullKey, '^[a-zA-Z0-9]+_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$'); split on '_', assert parts[0] == TokenPrefix, len(parts[1]) == 8, len(parts[2]) == 32; assert parts[1] == returned KeyID

property_tests.description: For any valid expiresDays value, ExpiresAt is nil iff expiresDays==0, and equals created_at + N*24h within tolerance for non-zero values.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each of {0, 30, 60, 90}: call GenerateAPIKey with that expiresDays and a unique userID; record before/after timestamps
property_tests.invariant_check: expiresDays==0 → result.ExpiresAt==nil AND db.expires_at IS NULL; expiresDays in {30,60,90} → result.ExpiresAt != nil AND abs(result.ExpiresAt - (before + expiresDays*24h)) < 2 seconds; db.expires_at matches result.ExpiresAt within 1 second

property_tests.description: For any response from GET /api/v1/user/keys, no element in the JSON array contains secret, secret_hash, key, or expires_days fields.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any user with between 0 and 10 keys (mix of active, expired, revoked), call GET /api/v1/user/keys
property_tests.invariant_check: Parse JSON array; for each object: assert set(obj.keys()) is a subset of {key_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at}; assert 'secret' not in obj; assert 'secret_hash' not in obj; assert 'key' not in obj; assert 'expires_days' not in obj

property_tests.description: For any GenerateAPIKey call with *sql.DB that fails after the revocation UPDATE, the prior key's revoked_at remains NULL (atomic rollback invariant).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any user with 0 or 1 existing keys: force all INSERT attempts to fail (via pre-inserted colliding key_ids or mock errors); call GenerateAPIKey with *sql.DB
property_tests.invariant_check: If user had a prior key (revoked_at IS NULL before call): after GenerateAPIKey returns error, query db and assert prior key still has revoked_at IS NULL; assert total row count for user is unchanged

property_tests.description: For any GET /api/v1/user/keys response with at least two keys, the created_at values are non-increasing (ORDER BY created_at DESC contract).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any user with between 2 and 10 keys (any mix of active/expired/revoked), call GET /api/v1/user/keys
property_tests.invariant_check: Parse JSON array; assert len(body) >= 2; for each adjacent pair (body[i], body[i+1]): assert parseTime(body[i].created_at) >= parseTime(body[i+1].created_at)

property_tests.description: GenerateAPIKey retry loop is bounded: the retry count for key_id collision never exceeds 3, ensuring termination under worst-case conditions.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Force exactly K unique constraint violations on INSERT (K in {1, 2, 3, 4}) and call GenerateAPIKey; count the total number of INSERT attempts
property_tests.invariant_check: For K <= 2: GenerateAPIKey succeeds on attempt K+1; total INSERT calls == K+1; assert total INSERT calls <= 3. For K >= 3: GenerateAPIKey returns non-nil error; assert total INSERT calls == 3 (loop terminates after exactly 3 attempts, never more)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a crypto/rand.Read() failure during a retry attempt (key_id collision scenario) is immediately fatal with no further retries.
preconditions: internal/keys package compiled with injectable rand reader
SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
Mock rand: returns valid bytes on first call (triggers collision via pre-inserted key_id), then fails on second call
edge_case_tests.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns non-nil error after the second rand call fails; no further retry attempts; no rows inserted for user-E1
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mockRand.failOnCall = 2  // succeed on call 1 (collide), fail on call 2
preInsertCollisionKey(db)
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-E1', 90, logger)
assert err != nil
assert result == nil
assert mockRand.callCount == 2  // stopped immediately, no more attempts
rowCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', 'user-E1')
assert rowCount == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when called with *sql.DB and the INSERT fails after the revocation UPDATE, the internal transaction is rolled back and the prior key's revoked_at remains NULL.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-E2 has one active key with key_id='priorkey', revoked_at IS NULL
A mechanism to exhaust all 3 INSERT retry attempts (pre-insert 3 rows that would collide with generated key_ids)
edge_case_tests.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns non-nil error; 'priorkey' row still has revoked_at IS NULL (transaction was rolled back); no new key row exists for user-E2 beyond 'priorkey'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
forceAllInsertFailures(db, 3)
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-E2', 90, logger)
assert err != nil
priorRow = db.queryRow('SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'priorkey')
assert priorRow.revoked_at == NULL
totalRows = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', 'user-E2')
assert totalRows == 1

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey called for a first-time user (no prior keys) silently handles zero-rows-affected revocation UPDATE and successfully inserts the new key.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-E3 has no existing rows in api_keys
edge_case_tests.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns (*APIKeyResult, nil); one new key row inserted for user-E3 with revoked_at IS NULL; no error from zero-rows-affected UPDATE
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-E3', 90, logger)
assert err == nil
assert result != nil
assert result.KeyID != ''
rowCount = queryCount('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-E3')
assert rowCount == 1

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a non-constraint-violation database error during INSERT causes GenerateAPIKey to return a non-nil error immediately without any retry.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
A mock db.Executor is injected that returns a generic (non-unique-constraint) database error on ExecContext for INSERT
edge_case_tests.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns non-nil error immediately; no retry attempted (INSERT called exactly once); surfaces as HTTP 500 'internal server error' to callers
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mockDB.insertError = genericDBError
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(mockDB, 'user-E4', 90, logger)
assert err != nil
assert result == nil
assert mockDB.insertCallCount == 1  // no retries for non-constraint errors

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey uses type assertion (not interface methods or wrapper types) to distinguish *sql.DB from *sql.Tx for transaction management.
preconditions: internal/keys package source code is available
edge_case_tests.expected: Source code of GenerateAPIKey contains type assertion pattern 'if _, ok := tx.(*sql.DB); ok'; no wrapper type or additional interface is introduced for transaction detection
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
source = readFile('internal/keys/generate.go')
assert contains(source, '_, ok := tx.(*sql.DB)')
assert not contains(source, 'type txWrapper')
assert not contains(source, 'type TxDetector')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that after all 3 key_id collision retry attempts fail with unique constraint violations, GenerateAPIKey returns an error surfaced as HTTP 500.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
Test harness can force all 3 generated key_ids to collide with pre-existing rows
edge_case_tests.expected: GenerateAPIKey returns non-nil error after exactly 3 INSERT attempts; calling handler returns HTTP 500 'internal server error'; no new active key for user-E6

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the ETag derivation query returns NULL (no keys), GET /api/v1/user/keys returns HTTP 200 with [] and no ETag header.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-E7 has no keys in api_keys
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-E7'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 200; response body is '[]'; ETag header is absent; If-None-Match is not evaluated
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user_E7_key, headers={'If-None-Match': '"some-etag"'})
assert resp.status == 200

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that revoking a key invalidates the prior ETag so a subsequent GET with If-None-Match returns HTTP 200 (not 304).
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-E8 has one active key
Auth middleware injects user_id='user-E8'
edge_case_tests.expected: First GET: HTTP 200 with ETag. After DELETE. Third GET with original ETag: HTTP 200 (not 304), confirming ETag was invalidated by revocation
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user_E8_key)
assert resp1.status == 200

DELETE('/api/v1/user/keys/'+keyID, auth=user_E8_key)

resp2 = GET('/api/v1/user/keys', auth=user_E8_key, headers={'If-None-Match': originalETag})
assert resp2.status == 200
assert resp2.headers.get('ETag') != originalETag

resp = POST('/api/v1/user/keys/'+keyID+'/refresh', auth=userE10APIKey)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that GenerateAPIKey sets revoked_at on an expired-but-not-revoked key during the pre-INSERT revocation UPDATE.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with api_keys table
User user-E15 has a key with expires_at='2020-01-01T00:00:00Z' (past) and revoked_at IS NULL
No other keys for user-E15
edge_case_tests.expected: After GenerateAPIKey: the expired key has revoked_at set (not NULL); the new key is the only row with revoked_at IS NULL
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
expiredKeyID = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-E15').key_id
result, err = GenerateAPIKey(db, 'user-E15', 90, logger)
assert err == nil

newActiveRow = db.queryRow('SELECT key_id FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', 'user-E15')

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test: OAuth callback creates a first API key for a new user via GenerateAPIKey within an existing *sql.Tx transaction, verifying the full happy-path flow.
smoke_tests.trigger: Simulate the OAuth callback calling apikit.GenerateAPIKey(tx, userID, 90, logger) with an active *sql.Tx that also upserts the user
expected_effects: GenerateAPIKey returns (*APIKeyResult, nil) with FullKey, KeyID, SecretHash, ExpiresAt populated
After tx.Commit(): exactly one row in api_keys for the user with revoked_at IS NULL
Row secret_hash == hex(sha256(secret from FullKey))
Row expires_at approximately now+90*24h
One INFO log entry emitted with user_id and key_id fields
FullKey matches ^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test: Authenticated user lists API keys with ETag caching — first request returns HTTP 200 with ETag, second with If-None-Match returns HTTP 304.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send GET /api/v1/user/keys with no If-None-Match, then send again with the returned ETag in If-None-Match
mockable: Auth middleware (inject user_id directly via context)
expected_effects: First request: HTTP 200 with JSON array containing key metadata; ETag header present; Cache-Control: no-store
Response objects contain only key_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at
Second request with matching If-None-Match: HTTP 304 with empty body
No secret or secret_hash fields in response

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test: User refreshes an active API key via POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh, verifying the new secret is returned and the DB is updated in-place.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id/refresh authenticated with a valid API key credential for an active, non-expired, non-revoked key
mockable: Auth middleware (inject user_id and credential type=API key via context)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 with JSON body containing key (full key string), key_id (unchanged), expires_at (recalculated or null)
New secret portion is 32 alphanumeric characters
DB row has updated secret_hash matching hex(sha256(new_secret))
DB row created_at updated to approximately now()
DB row expires_at recalculated from stored expires_days
Row count in api_keys for user unchanged
INFO log entry emitted with user_id and key_id

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test: User revokes their own active API key via DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id, verifying HTTP 200 and revoked_at set in DB.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send DELETE /api/v1/user/keys/:key_id authenticated with a valid API key or PAT with keys:manage for an active key belonging to the authenticated user
expected_effects: HTTP 200 with JSON body {key_id: <key_id>, revoked_at: <RFC3339 UTC>}
DB row for key_id has revoked_at set to approximately now()
INFO log entry emitted with user_id and key_id
Cache-Control: no-store present in response

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test: GenerateAPIKey called with *sql.DB when a prior active key exists — prior key is revoked and new key inserted atomically.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call GenerateAPIKey(db, userID, 30, logger) passing *sql.DB directly for a user with one existing active key
expected_effects: Prior active key's revoked_at is set to approximately now()
New key is inserted with the correct key_id, secret_hash, expires_days=30, expires_at=now+720h
Exactly one row with revoked_at IS NULL for the user after call
GenerateAPIKey returns (*APIKeyResult, nil) with FullKey, KeyID, SecretHash, ExpiresAt
Internal transaction committed successfully
INFO log entry emitted with user_id and key_id
